name: first-revenue-analysis
title: Revenue by product type
description: The kit's first guided workflow: which product type generated the most revenue, answered with inspect-before-run discipline and independent validation. Runs against the bundled TPC-H sample. Serves as the template for saving your own workflows.
question: Which product type generated the most revenue?

steps.description: Ground the assistant in the real schema before asking anything
steps.prompt: What schemas and tables are available in my Exasol database? For the tables in the TPCH schema, describe their columns and how they relate to each other.

steps.description: Ask the question with the inspect-before-run instruction
steps.prompt: Which product type generated the most revenue? Show me the SQL you intend to run and explain it before executing.
review_checklist: Which columns define revenue? (kit convention: L_EXTENDEDPRICE * (1 - L_DISCOUNT))
Is tax (L_TAX) included? (the convention above excludes it)
Are returned line items (L_RETURNFLAG = 'R') subtracted (net) or included (gross)?
Any filters applied (e.g. an O_ORDERDATE range)?

steps.description: The SQL approved during the ask step. In TPC-H a part's P_TYPE is the closest thing to a product category; revenue is summed from LINEITEM joined to PART. Column names follow data/data-dictionary.md.
steps.sql:
SELECT p.P_TYPE,
       SUM(l.L_EXTENDEDPRICE * (1 - l.L_DISCOUNT)) AS REVENUE
FROM   TPCH.LINEITEM l
JOIN   TPCH.PART p ON p.P_PARTKEY = l.L_PARTKEY
GROUP  BY p.P_TYPE
ORDER  BY REVENUE DESC
LIMIT  10

steps.description: Independent reproduction outside the assistant - same SQL, direct database path
Rerun any time with the validate command above; results are comparable run over run because the SQL is fixed and reviewed.

outcome.deliverable: Ranked product types (PART.P_TYPE) by revenue, verified through two independent paths
trust_properties: SQL reviewed by a human before execution
Read-only database access for the assistant
Result reproduced outside the assistant with exapump
Workflow captured in this file for rerun
